# descope_resource_policy (Resource)

Manages the access policy connecting one Descope inbound application to one API resource.

```terraform
resource "descope_resource_policy" "example" {
  project_id     = descope_project.example.id
  application_id = descope_inbound_app.example.id
  resource_id    = "RSxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

  user_access_scopes   = ["read"]
  client_access_scopes = ["read", "write"]
}
```

Import an existing policy with its project, application, and resource IDs:

```shell
terraform import descope_resource_policy.example Pxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx/APxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx/RSxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `application_id` (String) The inbound application ID referenced by this policy.
- `project_id` (String) The Descope project ID containing the application and resource.
- `resource_id` (String) The API resource ID referenced by this policy.

### Optional

- `all_client_scopes` (Boolean) Whether clients receive all scopes exposed by the resource. Defaults to `false`.
- `all_user_scopes` (Boolean) Whether authenticated users receive all scopes exposed by the resource. Defaults to `false`.
- `client_access_scopes` (Set of String) The resource scopes granted to clients.
- `user_access_scopes` (Set of String) The resource scopes granted to authenticated users.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The provider identity, composed as `application_id/resource_id`.
